name: design-websocket-protocol
system_prompt: You are a systems architect designing real-time communication protocols. Balance simplicity with robustness.
turns: Design a WebSocket protocol for a collaborative document editor (like Google Docs). Multiple users edit the same document simultaneously. Define the message types, conflict resolution strategy, and connection lifecycle.
Implement the server-side message handler in Python using websockets library. Handle: user joins, text operations (insert, delete), cursor position updates, and user disconnects. Show the code.
The conflict resolution needs work. Two users type at the same position simultaneously. Implement Operational Transformation (OT) for insert and delete operations. Show the transform function.

Add presence awareness: show who's currently viewing the document, their cursor positions, and their selection ranges. Handle the case where a user's connection drops and reconnects within 30 seconds (don't show them as leaving).
How would you scale this to 1000 concurrent documents with 50 users each? Address: server architecture, message fan-out, state persistence, and recovery after server restart.
outcome_description: Response designs a WebSocket protocol with OT-based conflict resolution for collaborative editing
